Off Calendar Feb-08-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jennifer Alesio, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Roger Ross, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT GARDNER DENVER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Gardner Denver, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is taken off calendar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Ross was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. Defendant's separate statement fails to demonstrate that it propounded contention discovery and that plaintiffs' responses thereto were factually devoid. This precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Moreover, the deposition testimony and declaration of Mr. Ross and the declaration of Dr. Bruch create triable issues whether Mr. Ross was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant and whether that exposure was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to Mr. Ross' asbestos-related disease. Defendant's alternative motion for summary adjudication is off calendar since plaintiffs represent that they are withdrawing their claim for punitive damages, which is the only issue presented for adjudication. Order signed in open court.Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
